name: Chinese: Beef with Green Pepper (Lot Tzu Ngow)
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Chinese black beans (Fermented black beans)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut steak on the diagonal into 1/4-inch slices each 2 inches long.
2. Mix 1 teaspoon cornstarch, 1 teaspoon soy sauce, 2 teaspoons rice wine, 1/4 teaspoon oil, and 1/4 teaspoon sugar in a bowl.
3. Pour this mixture over beef, mix well, and let stand for 15 minutes.
4. Cut peppers in half, remove seeds.
5. Cut into 1/4-inch long strips.
6. Smash, peel, and mince garlic.
7. Peel and mince ginger root.
8. In a cup, mix together 4 teaspoons cornstarch, 1 cup chicken broth, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, and 1/4 teaspoon sugar.
9. Wash black beans and drain.
COOKING:
1. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil with 1/4 teaspoon salt over high heat.
2. Add beef and stir-fry for 1 minute. Remove beef from the pan and set aside.
3. Clean the pan.
4. Heat the other tablespoon of oil over high heat.
5. Add mashed black beans, garlic, and ginger; stir-fry for about 1 minute.
6. Add green pepper and onion. Stir-fry for 2 minutes. (The pepper slices should remain crisp.)
7. Return beef to the skillet and add the cornstarch mixture.
8. Cook and stir until thickened, about 1 minute. Serve.
